message:
feat: Add 3-way merge infrastructure for robust JSONL synchronization

Implement the foundational types and algorithms for 3-way merge between
SQLite (local) and JSONL (external) states, enabling proper conflict
detection and resolution during sync operations.

## New Types (src/sync/mod.rs)

ConflictType enum:
- DeleteVsModify: Issue modified in one source, deleted in other
- ConvergentCreation: Same ID created independently with different content

MergeResult enum:
- NoAction: Issue doesn't exist in any source
- Keep(Issue): Keep the specified version
- KeepWithNote(Issue, String): Keep with explanation of merge decision
- Delete: Remove the issue
- Conflict(ConflictType): Requires manual resolution

MergeContext struct:
- base: HashMap of issues at last known common state
- left: HashMap of current SQLite/local issues
- right: HashMap of current JSONL/external issues
- all_issue_ids(): Collect unique IDs across all three states

MergeReport struct:
- kept: Issues that were created or updated
- deleted: Issues that were removed
- conflicts: Detected conflicts requiring attention
- tombstone_protected: Issues protected from resurrection
- notes: Merge decision explanations
- has_conflicts(): Quick check for any conflicts
- total_actions(): Count of changes made

ConflictResolution enum:
- PreferLocal: Always keep SQLite version
- PreferExternal: Always keep JSONL version
- PreferNewer: Use updated_at timestamp as tiebreaker (default)
- Manual: Report conflicts without auto-resolution

## Core Algorithm: merge_issue()

Implements proper 3-way merge logic for each issue:

Case 1: Only in base (deleted both sides) -> Delete
Case 2: Only in left (new local) -> Keep
Case 3: Only in right (new external) -> Keep
Case 4: Base+left, deleted in right:
  - If left modified after base -> conflict or keep (strategy dependent)
  - If left unchanged -> Delete (accept external deletion)
Case 5: Base+right, deleted in left:
  - If right modified after base -> conflict or keep (strategy dependent)
  - If right unchanged -> Delete (accept local deletion)
Case 6: All three exist:
  - Compare content_hash to detect changes
  - Neither changed -> Keep left
  - Only left changed -> Keep left
  - Only right changed -> Keep right
  - Both changed -> Apply resolution strategy
Case 7: Left+right only (convergent creation):
  - Same content_hash -> Keep left
  - Different -> Apply resolution strategy
Case 8: None exist -> NoAction

## Bug Fix: Import Hash Recording

Fixed import_from_jsonl() to record export hashes for ALL imported issues,
not just those that result in Insert or Update actions. Previously, issues
that matched existing records by content hash (CollisionAction::Skip) would
not have their hashes recorded, leading to false dirty detection on
subsequent syncs.

The fix:
- Always compute final_id from collision result, not action result
- Record hash for all issues regardless of action taken
- Use existing_id for Match collisions, new ID for NewIssue

Test update (tests/jsonl_import_export.rs):
- Call finalize_export() to persist hashes after export
- This ensures the test properly validates hash round-tripping

## Comprehensive Test Suite

13 new unit tests covering:
- test_merge_new_local_issue_kept
- test_merge_new_external_issue_kept
- test_merge_deleted_both_sides
- test_merge_deleted_external_unmodified_local
- test_merge_deleted_external_modified_local
- test_merge_deleted_local_modified_external
- test_merge_only_local_modified
- test_merge_only_external_modified
- test_merge_both_modified_prefer_newer
- test_merge_both_modified_prefer_local
- test_merge_convergent_creation_same_content
- test_merge_convergent_creation_different_content
- test_merge_neither_changed
- test_merge_context_all_issue_ids
- test_merge_report_has_conflicts
- test_merge_report_total_actions

Each test uses fixed timestamps via fixed_time_merge() helper for
deterministic behavior.

## Future Integration

This infrastructure prepares for:
1. Full 3-way sync: base state tracking via git commits
2. Conflict UI: Present conflicts to user for resolution
3. Merge strategies: Per-project or per-sync configuration
4. Audit trail: Record merge decisions in events

The merge_issue() function is pure and stateless, making it easy to
test and compose into larger sync workflows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
